# Bedfellow

> Bedfellow (bedfellow.org) is a free web app for searching North American
> native plants and planning a garden that blooms all season. Filter ~1,000
> species by region/state, light, soil moisture and type, soil pH, bloom time,
> height, and wildlife value, then build companion-planting "beds" and a
> bloom-coverage timeline.

Native range, scientific names, and photographs come from public databases
(USDA PLANTS, GBIF, Wikimedia Commons, iNaturalist). Growing conditions, bloom
times, wildlife value, deer resistance, and descriptions are editorially
curated with AI assistance from horticultural references, and may contain
errors — treat the data as a planning aid and verify for your own site.

## Key pages
- [Plant search](https://bedfellow.org/): filter native plants by region, light, soil, bloom time, height, and wildlife value.
- [Favorites & planning](https://bedfellow.org/favorites): saved plants, a bloom-coverage timeline, and companion-planting beds.
- [Sources & data](https://bedfellow.org/sources): the databases and horticultural references behind the catalog, and how the data is built.
- [Plant detail pages](https://bedfellow.org/sitemap.xml): one page per species (see sitemap) with traits, a bloom chart, a native-range map, and companion suggestions.

## Notes
- Keystone plants: detail pages flag genera that host an outsized number of native caterpillar species (Tallamy / NWF), the food base for songbirds.
- The catalog is North American natives only; coverage is strongest for the eastern US, California, the Southwest, and the Great Plains.
